message.content: The user has invoked you directly. Begin a new session as James. Follow your startup procedure: read your notes files, then greet the user and ask what they'd like to work through today.

content.content:
1→# My Notes for James
     2→
     3→This file is read by the James agent at the start of each session.
     4→Write anything here you want James to be aware of: meeting notes, observations while developing, architectural decisions already made, context about the project.
     5→
     6→The James agent will never modify this file.
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→<!-- Add your notes below -->
    11→
    12→
    13→Date: 21 March 2026
    14→Currently have two primary open issues with James:
    15→
    16→1) BIG. The Enrichment Definition / Enrichment Pipeline system. 
    17→- We have enrichments that will be essentially starting off with the same steps: referencing a document, getting all the text chunks, and then performing a task on these text chunks.
    18→- The two steps: referencing a document and gathering the text chunks can then be taken away from the enrichments that use this system, which should make the enrichments simpler for the next person who wants to develop an enrichment like this.
    19→	- sample extra enrichments would be like extracting all the job titles from text, or drug names. We have no plan to do this right now.
    20→- James says this needs to use some kind of an interface which would define the functions that each of these would use. I think this would be something like:
    21→	- parsing the text: takes in the text, performs its job (such as extracting all temporal entities)
    22→	- writing to neo: writes whatever nodes to Neo (such as all the requirement nodes)
    23→	- then, whatever other functions are necessary are just local to that one extractor?
    24→- the actual extractions don't matter right now, and we don't erally care about them. The architecture is what is most important
    25→- The current implementation is just a broad-stroke first pass which I am not happy with or proud of
    26→- The enrichments should register with whatever EnrichmentRegistration system I put in place, they should not be explicitly called from the pipeline
    27→	- The pipeline should somehow iterate over all registered enrichments
    28→- I need to have a diagram and a small example for my meeting tomorrow
    29→
    30→2) SMALL. The Testing System.
    31→- I implemented a really crappy and quick testing suite for each module for James. 
    32→- I need to be able to explain this testing system
    33→	- What the coverage report means
    34→	- Where its lacking
    35→	- Why it's done how I did it
    36→- Essentially I just need to explain and have like a 15 minute conversation about what it means

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

     7→

     9→<!-- One entry per topic currently in play.
    10→     Format:
    11→       [YYYY-MM-DD] Topic name: brief description of where things stand
    12→       [DONE YYYY-MM-DD] Topic name: one-line outcome summary
    13→
    14→     Pruning rules:
    15→       - Mark complete with [DONE YYYY-MM-DD] and condense to one line immediately.
    16→       - Topics not touched in 14+ days: condense to a single line.
    17→       - Topics and completed items are NEVER removed — they stay as one-liners indefinitely. -->
    18→
    19→## Session log
    20→<!-- One entry per session, most recent first.
    21→     Format: [YYYY-MM-DD] Brief summary of what was discussed and any decisions reached.
    22→
    23→     Pruning rule: entries older than 30 days are removed. -->
    24→
    25→## Learning journey

    27→     Format:

    29→     Status: introduced | practicing | understood
    30→     'Understood' entries are kept permanently as one-liners.
    31→     'Introduced' and 'practicing' entries are condensed after 14 days but never removed. -->
    32→

content.text:
Right then. Good morning. Looks like this is our first session together, so we're starting with a clean slate on my end.

What would you like to work through today?
